purpose: PreToolUse hook on Bash that blocks bash commands whose `dashboards/{id}/...` path references differ from the agent's assigned dashboard. Companion to enforce-dashboard-isolation.sh which handles Edit/Write — this catches mkdir/cp/rm/echo/cat redirects that reference dashboards. Resolves assigned dashboard from SYNAPSE_DASHBOARD_ID env var (set by Electron) or `DASHBOARD ID:` directive between ===DASHBOARD_BINDING_START/END=== markers in the transcript. Greps the command for `dashboards/{id}` patterns and blocks if any referenced ID differs from the assignment. Fail-open: missing jq, no input, or no assignment all allow.
gotchas: Fail-open by design — raw CLI usage without SYNAPSE_DASHBOARD_ID and without binding markers is unblocked. Only enforces when an assignment is discoverable.
Validates dashboard ID shape (^(ide|[a-f0-9]{6}|dashboardN)$) before trusting it — prevents transcripts containing quoted text from injecting fake assignments
Pattern match `dashboards/[a-zA-Z0-9_]+` — won't catch dashboards/Some-Hyphenated-ID (no hyphens in regex)
Multiple dashboard refs in one command must ALL match the assignment — first mismatch blocks
patterns: PreToolUse Bash matcher with stdin tool_input.command
Defense in depth: paired with enforce-dashboard-isolation.sh (Edit|Write) for bash-level coverage
conventions: allow()/block() helper functions for consistent JSON output
set -o pipefail for safe error propagation
